prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear! A CPU is like the brain of your computer—it’s the little part inside that does all the thinking. When you click on a game or type a message, the CPU figures out what to do next, just like how I decide whether to bake cookies or go for a walk. It doesn’t store things—that’s what memory and hard drives are for—but it makes everything happen by following step-by-step instructions. Even though it’s tiny, it works super fast, faster than I can even imagine! Think of it as the helpful cook in the kitchen of your computer, always stirring the pot so your digital soup comes out just right. Isn’t that clever?
